mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days as investors price in future rate hikes.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong labor market dynamics, driving corporate earnings optimism and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in <30% of instances where PAYEMS rises >100k and UNRATE falls >0.5pp simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST) combined with expanding GDP (GDPC1) indicates robust demand conditions, creating positive momentum in equity markets over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are negative when HOUST >650 and GDPC1 growth exceeds 3% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) alongside declining real sales (RSAFS) signals overcapacity and profit margin pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day SPX returns occur in <20% of instances where INDPRO rises >2% and RSAFS falls >1%.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5 percentage point decline in unemployment (UNRATE) over one month signals improving labor market efficiency, boosting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative following UNRATE declines exceeding 0.5pp in 30-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 650 thousand units signal strong demand for construction, driving related sector performance and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative when HOUST exceeds 650 for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A 10 basis point increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals monetary tightening, reducing liquidity and creating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive despite FEDFUNDS rising by >10 bps in 30-day windows.
